name: GoReleaser (Go Release Automation)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); GoReleaser Pro available with extra features

auth.notes: GITHUB_TOKEN / GITLAB_TOKEN / GITEA_TOKEN env vars for publishing releases

performance.notes: Build + package + publish pipeline; depends on project size and artifact count

agent_readiness.notes: Go release automation tool. Single YAML config (.goreleaser.yaml) defines cross-compilation targets, archive formats, checksums, Docker images, homebrew taps, RPM/deb packages, GitHub/GitLab/Gitea releases. Cross-compiles to linux/darwin/windows across amd64/arm64/386. Integrates with Syft for SBOM, Cosign for signing, Nix/Scoop/Winget. Pro version adds nightly builds, split/merge archives, announce hooks. Standard for Go project releases.

gotchas: Requires tagged commit for full release (use --snapshot for local testing)
Cross-compilation requires CGO_ENABLED=0 or Docker builders for CGO projects
Config YAML has evolved significantly across versions — check migration guides
GitHub token scope must include repo permissions for release creation
best_when: Go project release automation (cross-compile + publish in one command)
Multi-target binary distribution (homebrew, scoop, apt, rpm)
CI/CD release pipelines with artifact signing and SBOM generation
Open source Go projects needing professional release infrastructure
avoid_when: Simple projects that just need git tag + gh release create
Projects requiring highly custom release logic
summary: GoReleaser is the standard release automation tool for Go projects. Single YAML config handles cross-compilation (linux/darwin/windows, amd64/arm64), archive creation, checksum generation, Docker images, Homebrew taps, and GitHub/GitLab release publishing. Supports SBOM (Syft), signing (Cosign), and package managers (apt/rpm/nix/scoop). Essential for any Go project with multi-platform distribution needs.
